title: Macbeth
pairs: What genre is Macbeth?
Macbeth is a tragedy by William Shakespeare.
What kind of play is Macbeth?
Macbeth is a tragedy.
Did Shakespeare write Macbeth?
Yes, Macbeth was written by William Shakespeare.
Where is Macbeth set?
Macbeth is set in Scotland.
What is the setting of Macbeth?
The setting of Macbeth is Scotland.
In what location does Macbeth take place?
Macbeth takes place in Scotland.
Who is Macbeth in Macbeth?
Macbeth is a Scottish general who murders the king to seize power.
What is Macbeth's role in Macbeth?
Macbeth is a Scottish general who murders the king to seize power.
Describe Macbeth in Macbeth.
Macbeth is a Scottish general who murders the king to seize power.
Who is Lady Macbeth in Macbeth?
Lady Macbeth is Macbeth's ambitious wife who urges him to kill Duncan.
What is Lady Macbeth's role in Macbeth?
Lady Macbeth is Macbeth's ambitious wife who urges him to kill Duncan.
Describe Lady Macbeth in Macbeth.
Lady Macbeth is Macbeth's ambitious wife who urges him to kill Duncan.
Who is Duncan in Macbeth?

Duncan is the King of Scotland, murdered by Macbeth.

Duncan is the King of Scotland, murdered by Macbeth.
Who is Banquo in Macbeth?
Banquo is a general and Macbeth's friend, murdered on Macbeth's orders.

Banquo is a general and Macbeth's friend, murdered on Macbeth's orders.
Describe Banquo in Macbeth.
Banquo is a general and Macbeth's friend, murdered on Macbeth's orders.

Macduff is a Scottish nobleman who ultimately kills Macbeth.

Macduff is a Scottish nobleman who ultimately kills Macbeth.

Malcolm is Duncan's son and the rightful heir to the throne.

The Witches is three supernatural figures who prophesy Macbeth's rise and fall.

Macbeth's wife is Lady Macbeth.

What are the main themes of Macbeth?
The main themes of Macbeth include ambition, guilt, power, fate, and the supernatural.
What does Macbeth explore?
Macbeth explores themes of ambition, guilt, power, fate, and the supernatural.
Is ambition a theme in Macbeth?
Yes, ambition is a central theme in Macbeth.
Is guilt a theme in Macbeth?
Yes, guilt is a central theme in Macbeth.
Is power a theme in Macbeth?
Yes, power is a central theme in Macbeth.
What do the witches prophesy to Macbeth?
They prophesy that Macbeth will become King of Scotland.
How does Macbeth become king?
Macbeth murders King Duncan in his sleep and blames the guards.
Why does Macbeth have Banquo killed?
Macbeth kills Banquo because the witches prophesied that Banquo's descendants would be kings.
What is Lady Macbeth's role in the murder?
Lady Macbeth plans the murder of Duncan and pressures Macbeth to carry it out.
What is Lady Macbeth's fate?
Overwhelmed by guilt, Lady Macbeth loses her sanity and dies, likely by suicide.
What does Macbeth see at the banquet?
Macbeth sees the ghost of Banquo sitting at the banquet table, which terrifies him.
Who kills Macbeth?
Macduff kills Macbeth in single combat after Malcolm's army invades Scotland.
What is the significance of Birnam Wood?
The witches said Macbeth would fall when Birnam Wood moved to Dunsinane, which happens when soldiers carry branches.
How does Macbeth end?
Macbeth is killed by Macduff, and Malcolm is restored as the rightful King of Scotland.
What is the ending of Macbeth?
Macbeth is killed by Macduff, and Malcolm is restored as the rightful King of Scotland.
Why is Macbeth a tragedy?
Macbeth is a tragedy because macbeth is killed by macduff, and malcolm is restored as the rightful king of scotland.
Who says "Out, damned spot!" in Macbeth?
Lady Macbeth says this famous line in Macbeth.
What is a famous line from Macbeth?
A famous line from Macbeth is: "Out, damned spot!"
